message:
feat(fwc): block workflow execution when compiled truth is non-success and fix Unknown aggregation

Introduces blocking workflow availability gates so that capsules and
tasks surface the actual availability state (unknown, unavailable,
denied) instead of proceeding to execution and producing misleading
"execution-error" statuses.

workflow.rs (+323 lines):
- blocking_workflow_availability(): inspects compiled workflow truth and
  returns the availability tag when non-success, blocking execution
- ready_for_execution(): returns false when blocking_workflow_availability
  is non-None
- derive_capsule_status(): uses availability tag (e.g., "unknown",
  "unavailable") instead of generic "execution-error"
- build_task_next_actions(): emits "do not advance or run" guidance when
  blocked, omitting task run/advance from suggested actions
- Comprehensive tests: blocking gate, capsule status derivation,
  next_actions suppression, parse_command_availability round-trips,
  from_compiler non-authoritative invariant for all 7 availability
  variants (bead 29.8.4)

intent.rs (+256 lines):
- Fixed aggregate_compiled_step_availability: Unknown steps were silently
  ignored, so LiveRuntime + Unknown would report LiveRuntime. Now tracks
  saw_unknown and surfaces it with correct precedence
- compiled_workflow_truth: new match arm for "has unknown steps" case
  with explanatory message
- Tests: aggregate_unknown_propagation, explanation message coverage,
  from_compiler invariant for all 7 variants

main.rs (+40 lines):
- Fixed --offline flag placement in ~10 tests: moved from global flag
  position to after subcommand name per CLI contract
- Updated "execution-error" → "unavailable" assertions for capsule
  and task status to match new workflow truth blocking behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
